risk_flags: hole pattern alignment on large part

part_summary.notes: Large heavy flange (815 kg). Irregular thickness with angled sections (44°, 52°). Multiple concentric bolt hole patterns on both faces. Engrave part number '1SI0574AM122'. Remove sharp edges/burrs. Imperial dimensions, general tolerances apply.

machining_features.description: Large thru holes Ø1.000 in (20x, O-ring?)

dimensions.depth_mm: THRU min depth for complete circle

machining_features.description: Thread hole chamfers Ø0.563 in x 82 deg

general_notes: Large machine capacity required (>111 in dia, 815 kg).
Precise fixturing for hole pattern alignment across large spans.
Verify all PCD dimensions and hole positions from detailed views.
Potential for distortion due to size and material removal.

scenarios.rationale: Primary routing using the large 4-axis CNC boring mill for all precision machining operations grouped into 3 setups to access multiple faces and features (top/bottom/periphery). Includes facing, contouring, drilling, boring, threading, engraving, and chamfering. Final deburr in fabrication bay. Matches part summary (3 setups, 6 machined faces, high hole count).
assumptions: Raw stock pre-cut to machine envelope limits (~2500x1500mm plan view).
4th axis indexing used for peripheral features.
Probing used for hole pattern alignment.
Imperial tolerances and surface finish met with general parameters.
unknowns: Fixturing details for 815kg part stability and repeatability.
Potential thermal distortion from heavy cuts on large thin flange.
Exact hole position true positions beyond general tolerances.

steps.description: Rough and semi-finish face mill top/bottom faces (F001), external contoured profile/steps/shoulders (F002/F012), angled faces (F011). Large area removal.
Drill thru holes (F004:16xØ17mm, F005:19xØ14.3mm, F006:20xØ25.4mm), bore blind holes (F007:9xØ12.8x12.7mm deep). PCD patterns.
Thread milled/tapped holes (F008:55x1/2-13 UNC thru, F009:1x3/8-18 NPT), chamfers (F010:56x), engraving (F003: part#).
Manual/semi-auto deburr all edges, holes, features (ENLEVER ARETES COUPANTES), light grind as needed.

scenarios.rationale: Plasma rough cuts outer profile from full-size plate stock to reduce CNC stock removal volume and cycle time. Followed by 3 CNC setups for precision features. Lower risk for heavy roughing distortion, but adds transfer step.
assumptions: Plasma table accommodates ~2820mm dia flange via optimized nesting/rotation (diagonal fit feasible for circular shape).
~5mm stock left post-plasma for CNC finish allowance on OD/contour.
No significant plasma HAZ affecting machinability.
unknowns: Plasma cut time/quality on 60mm 1045 steel (near max thickness).
Inter-process handling logistics for 800+kg rough blank.

steps.description: Plasma cut rough outer contoured profile from 2900x2900x60mm 1045 plate (perimeter ~4400mm, thick steel).
Finish face mill faces (F001), finish contour/steps/angled (F002/F011/F012). Reduced stock removal.
Drill thru holes (F004/F005/F006), bore blind (F007). PCD/patterns.
Thread/tap (F008/F009), chamfers (F010), engraving (F003).
Deburr edges, holes, plasma rough areas.

risk_flags: large envelope requires big machine capacity

part_summary.notes: Large rectangular plate with stepped contour along primary length. Oxy-cut rough stock implied. Holes likely on primary face. Tight general machining tolerances apply (±0.002" for dimensions). Chrome plating post-machining. Lower anvil smooth surface requires facing.

Stepped and radiused long edge contour (includes ~668mm dia arc and steps at multiple positions)
Drill for counterbore holes (thru or deep)
Edge chamfers (1/4" x 50°, others per details)
Multiple step shoulders along contoured edge

general_notes: General machining tolerances: ±1/16" for 1/X, ±0.005" for .xx, ±0.002" for .xxx, ±0.5° angles

scenarios.rationale: Plasma rough cut for efficient near-net shape blanking, followed by 2-setup CNC machining on large boring mill for precision features (top contour/holes, flip for bottom facing), then chrome plating. Matches part size, weight, features, and tolerances.
assumptions: Steel material CHT-100 plasma-compatible
2 CNC setups sufficient (top/bottom)
Chrome plating post-machining external or in-house
Imperial tolerances achievable on machine

steps.description: Plasma cut rough stock to estimated blank size (1020x250x70mm) with allowance for steps/contour
Setup 1: Face primary top anvil surface (F001), machine stepped/radiused long edge contour (F003,F009), counterbores/drills/taps/chamfers (F004-F008) on top face
Setup 2: Flip/reclamp, face bottom surface (F002)
Chrome plating 0.005" to 0.010" thick per spec (post-machining surface treatment)

scenarios.rationale: Fully CNC machined from oversized rectangular stock plate, avoiding plasma cut. Includes additional roughing time for contour. Suitable if plasma unavailable or for better edge quality.
assumptions: Oversized stock plate available (e.g., 1050x300x80mm)
Machine can handle roughing passes on contour without plasma pre-cut
Same 2 setups as baseline
unknowns: Additional vibration/chatter risk on rough contouring

Setup 2: Flip, face bottom (F002)

part_summary.notes: Irregular plate shape (anvil lateral right). Oxy-cut rough stock. Chrome plating required on 7 surfaces (0.005" to 0.010"). Weight: 13.437 lbm. Tight tolerances: ±0.002" on dimensions, hole positions.

machining_features.description: Irregular external profile with radii R 7.938" (201.7mm) and R 8.562" (217.4mm), curved edges
Primary facing operations on 7 chromed surfaces

machining_features.location: external edges per sections B-B, C-C, D, E

additional_operations.description: Chrome plating on 7 surfaces, 0.005" to 0.010" thick

general_notes: Projection: standard. General tolerances: USINAGE ±0.005"/.002", angles ±0.5°. Preparation/assembly tolerances looser. Mirror part: 803100-2401_mir also referenced but qty 1 each.
Multi-setup required for 7 faces and hole alignments. Risk: feature alignment across faces.

scenarios.rationale: Plasma rough cut followed by 3 CNC boring mill setups to handle multi-face machining and precision hole features, ending with chrome plating. Matches part complexity (7 faces, 8 holes, tight tolerances) and estimated 3 setups.
assumptions: Plasma table used as proxy for oxy-cut roughing
Chrome plating post-machining on all 7 surfaces
No material defects from cutting/plating
Handling includes load/unload per machine defaults
unknowns: Exact chrome plating cycle time and vendor lead
Potential distortion from plating requiring rework

steps.description: Plasma/oxy rough cut irregular anvil profile from plate stock (oversize 400x280x32mm)
CNC Setup 1: Face primary bottom surface, external contour (R7.938" & R8.562"), drill/chamfer 4x 11/16" holes + 82° countersink
CNC Setup 2: Flip/reclamp, face top surface, drill/chamfer 4x 13/32" holes + chamfers
CNC Setup 3: Side facing (remaining 5 chromed faces), edge chamfers (45°/65°)
Chrome plating on 7 surfaces (0.005"-0.010" thick)

scenarios.rationale: Single rough cut, then leverage 4-axis CNC boring mill with rotary indexing to reduce setups to 2 by accessing multiple faces without full reclamps. Higher setup time but lower total handling/inspection.
assumptions: 4-axis rotary B sufficient for side faces without 5th axis
No fixturing complexity penalty beyond 1.3x multiplier
Plating tolerance maintained post-machining
unknowns: Exact rotary indexing time for irregular shape

steps.description: Plasma/oxy rough cut to near-net (allows 4-axis fixturing)
CNC 4-Axis Setup 1: Bottom/top facing, full contour, all 8 holes + countersinks/chamfers (rotary for alignment)
CNC 4-Axis Setup 2: Rotary index for 5 side faces + edge chamfers
Chrome plating on 7 surfaces

scenarios.rationale: Plasma rough cut followed by 3 CNC boring mill setups to handle multi-face machining and precision hole features, ending with chrome plating. Matches part complexity (7 faces, 8 holes, tight tolerances) and estimated 3 setups.

drivers: High feature count (10 holes, 2 threaded holes, 2 bores, 1 pocket/slot)
risk_flags: Fixturing challenges for long thin bar

part_summary.notes: Long slender rectangular bar (key steel). Multiple precision holes along length. End bores and NPT threads. Careful fixturing required to prevent distortion/warping. Tight tolerances on hole positions (±0.002" on .xxx dims).

machining_features.description: Top and bottom long faces (facing operation)

general_notes: T = ÉPAISSEUR LA PLUS MINCE (thinnest thickness)
Slender profile: fixturing challenges, distortion risk

scenarios.rationale: Primary routing using large 4-axis CNC boring/milling machine suitable for long slender prismatic part with multiple precision holes, bores, threads, and slot. Features grouped into 3 logical setups to minimize re-fixturing while addressing fixturing challenges and tight tolerances. Total estimated cycle time aligns with feature estimates (~118 min).
assumptions: Oversized bar stock (530x38x30 mm) sawn to length prior to machining
Standard softjaw or modular fixturing prevents distortion
Carbide tools and coolant-through-spindle used
Material is machinable carbon key steel (density ~7.85 g/cm³)
No heat treatment required post-machining
unknowns: Exact fixturing design for slender profile
Tool life and breakage risk for deep bores

steps.description: Rough and finish facing of all external faces (top/bottom long faces, narrow sides, ends), chamfers, and air groove/slot (F001, F002, F007, F008, F009)
Drill 10x Ø9/32" through holes and countersink Ø19/32" x 82° (F003, F004) along centerline with positional tolerance ±0.002"
Bore 2x Ø39/64" x 2" deep at ends and tap 3/8-18 NPT x 13/32" deep (F005, F006)

scenarios.rationale: Split into 4 setups for improved hole positional accuracy and reduced distortion risk on slender part, with additional inspections. Same machine group. Higher setup/inspection time but lower risk.
assumptions: Additional torque plates or supports in fixturing
In-process probing for hole locations
Extended cycle times for conservative feeds/speeds

End bores and NPT threads (F005, F006)

part_summary.notes: Heavy bracket (~17kg / 37.39 lbs), likely forged steel, imperial units, multiple setups required, feature alignment risks, heavy fixturing

general_notes: Risks: multiple machining setups, feature alignment, heavy part fixturing

scenarios.rationale: Multi-setup machining on large 4-axis CNC boring mill to handle 5 machined faces, heavy fixturing, holes, bores, pockets/slots on forged steel bracket, followed by welding/assembly. Matches part size (152x127x38mm, 17kg), complexity drivers, and available machine capabilities.
assumptions: Forged steel blank provided near net shape
All machining on single machine group due to size/weight
Imperial units with standard tolerances except one high-precision bore (±0.001")
Welding is post-machining for final assembly
No 4th/5th axis needed beyond indexing

steps.description: Setup 1: Fixture blank, rough mill primary face, external contour, initial pocket/slot roughing
Setup 2: Flip/reclamp to secondary face, rough mill face 2, continue pocket/slot
Setup 3: Reposition for faces 3-4, rough/finish remaining faces, alignment checks
Setup 4: Drill 8 standard holes across faces
Setup 5: Bore/ream 4 holes including high-precision feature, final finishes
Welding and assembly operations

scenarios.rationale: Consolidated setups (4 total machining) using 4-axis indexing to minimize flips/reclamps, aggressive roughing, combined drilling/boring where possible, then welding. Reduces setup overhead for higher efficiency.
assumptions: 4-axis indexing allows access to multiple faces without full reclamps
High material removal optimized with large tools
Precision bore sequenced last to avoid damage
Similar to baseline but fewer interruptions

Setup 3: Combined drilling (8 holes) and rough boring
Setup 4: Finish boring/reaming high-precision hole, final inspection
Welding and light grinding/assembly

part_summary.notes: Imperial units. Weight 277.98 lbm. Remove sharp edges. Painting: mask threaded holes/machined surfaces, JC-JAUNE CATERPILLAR (5 GAL #4C6122 / 1 GAL #4C4200). Anti-rust on unpainted surfaces. ASME Y14.5M-2018.

general_notes: General tolerances: linear .XXX=±0.010"; angular .XX=±0.25°. Non-specified per note.

scenarios.rationale: Plasma cut rough profile from plate stock for efficient blanking given length and plasma envelope fit, followed by 4-setup machining on large 4-axis CNC boring mill per part summary to address fixturing challenges and feature density, concluded with masking and painting.
assumptions: Plate stock provided by customer or shop (44W steel, ~2900x160x60mm)
CNC boring mill accommodates 2850mm length via overhang/special fixturing (table 2000mm)
Painting in-house with specified Caterpillar yellow formula
Total cycle aligns with feature time estimates (~270min machining)
unknowns: Precise fixturing method for distortion control

steps.description: Plasma cut rough blank (perimeter profile, 50mm thick steel plate)
Setup 1: Mill primary top/bottom faces and initial flats (F001)
Setup 2: Mill sides, ends, contour, steps (F002, F003, F007)
Setup 3: Drill clearance holes, bore/ream, counterbores (F004, F005, F008)
Setup 4: Tap threaded holes (F006)
Post-machining: Deburr sharp edges, mask threads/machined areas, paint JC-JAUNE Caterpillar (5:1 mix), apply anti-rust agent

scenarios.rationale: Skip plasma for superior edge quality and tolerance control; rough mill contour from oversized plate on CNC boring mill using multiple roughing passes and 5 setups to mitigate length/distortion risks, suitable if plasma unavailable or quality premium required.
assumptions: Oversized plate stock fits table with multiple clamps/overhang
Extended roughing cycles acceptable (adds ~100min)
Same painting process
unknowns: Additional distortion from heat in roughing

steps.description: Rough saw/plate shear to approximate length if needed (manual)
Setup 1-2: Rough mill contour and faces from plate (extended for no plasma)
Setup 3: Finish mill faces, sides, steps (F001-F003, F007)
Setup 4: Holes - drill, bore, counterbore (F004, F005, F008)
Setup 5: Tap threads (F006)
Finishing: Deburr, mask, paint, anti-rust

Total cycle aligns with feature time estimates (~270min machining)
unknowns: Precise fixturing method for distortion control

steps.description: Plasma cut rough blank (perimeter profile, 50mm thick steel plate)
Setup 1: Mill primary top/bottom faces and initial flats (F001)
Setup 2: Mill sides, ends, contour, steps (F002, F003, F007)
Setup 3: Drill clearance holes, bore/ream, counterbores (F004, F005, F008)
Setup 4: Tap threaded holes (F006)
Post-machining: Deburr sharp edges, mask threads/machined areas, paint JC-JAUNE Caterpillar (5:1 mix), apply anti-rust agent

scenarios.rationale: Skip plasma for superior edge quality and tolerance control; rough mill contour from oversized plate on CNC boring mill using multiple roughing passes and 5 setups to mitigate length/distortion risks, suitable if plasma unavailable or quality premium required.
assumptions: Oversized plate stock fits table with multiple clamps/overhang
Extended roughing cycles acceptable (adds ~100min)
Same painting process
unknowns: Additional distortion from heat in roughing

steps.description: Rough saw/plate shear to approximate length if needed (manual)
Setup 1-2: Rough mill contour and faces from plate (extended for no plasma)
Setup 3: Finish mill faces, sides, steps (F001-F003, F007)
Setup 4: Holes - drill, bore, counterbore (F004, F005, F008)
Setup 5: Tap threads (F006)
Finishing: Deburr, mask, paint, anti-rust

part_summary.notes: Large thin aluminum plate with high hole density. Distortion risk during machining and anodizing. METRIC units. General tolerances: X: +/-1mm, X.X: +/-0.1mm, X.XX: +/-0.01mm, X.XXX: +/-0.001mm. Angles +/-1°, Concentricity 0.5mm, Break edges 0.13mm, Surface finish 3.2um.

scenarios.scenario_name: Baseline CNC Milling with Plasma Rough
scenarios.rationale: Plasma cut for efficient rough outline on large thin plate to minimize CNC stock removal, followed by two-setup CNC precision machining on large 4-axis boring mill for faces, contour, high-density holes and reaming, ending with clear anodize. Matches part size, feature density, and thin-wall distortion risks.
assumptions: Plasma heat distortion is minimal on 12mm stock
Anodize facility available with large rack capacity
Through holes drilled/reamed in two setups for access
Raw sheet stock available in 6061-T6
unknowns: Exact perimeter length for plasma time
Fixturing design for thin plate clamping

steps.description: Rough plasma cut irregular perimeter from 2050x1450x12mm sheet stock
Face top surface, mill perimeter contour with fillets, drill 7/9/12mm holes, partial ream 16mm holes, chamfer edges
Flip part, face bottom surface, finish ream 16mm holes to tolerance
Clear anodize all surfaces to 3.2um finish spec

scenarios.rationale: Machine entire part from rectangular raw stock without plasma, using two setups on large CNC boring mill. Suitable if plasma distortion risk is high, but increases CNC cycle time due to additional roughing of perimeter.
assumptions: Raw plate stock pre-sawn to near-net 2050x1450x12mm
Adequate vacuum/vacuum fixturing for thin plate
No need for plasma due to available stock cutting
unknowns: Additional roughing time for perimeter removal
Clamp mark mitigation on anodize surfaces

steps.description: Rough and finish face top to 6.35mm thick, rough/finish perimeter contour, drill all holes, partial ream, chamfer
Flip part, finish face bottom, complete ream holes from bottom side
Clear anodize all surfaces to 3.2um finish spec
